svg.title: Ground-Up New Build Trade Conflict Coordination Tree
svg.desc: Static coordination tree for ground-up construction showing phase-based trade conflicts and mitigation priorities.
svg.text: GROUND-UP NEW BUILD TRADE CONFLICT TREE
Use with the Trade Conflict Coordination Framework section to compare baseline interfaces against detected simultaneous-operation risks.

g.text: 1. Preconstruction / Controls
Project management, logistics, survey, and inspection conflicts ripple across every trade when sequence, access, and hold points are loose.
Typical pressure points: schedule control, laydown ownership, crane and delivery routes, disturbed layout marks, covered work before inspection.
Mitigation focus: freeze the master sequence, assign zone ownership, protect survey control, and enforce inspection gates before close-in.
Key interfaces: Superintendent | Safety / logistics | Survey / layout | Testing / inspection
2. Sitework / Civil
Civil work fronts clash when clearing, grading, shoring, underground utilities, paving, and landscape restoration overlap in the same corridor.
Typical pressure points: utility locates, excavation support, crane pads, backfill timing, paving before underground completion, final-grade damage.
Mitigation focus: phase corridor releases, verify utility as-builts before restoration, and keep finished surfaces protected from late logistics traffic.
Key interfaces: Clearing | Erosion control | Earthwork | Excavation / shoring | Underground utilities | Paving / landscaping
3. Structure / Building Shell
Foundations, embeds, structural steel, deck, masonry, roofing, waterproofing, facade, glazing, and fireproofing compete for access and closure rights.
Typical pressure points: pre-pour coordination, crane picks, opening locations, cure time, scaffold ownership, dry-in milestones, penetration conflicts.
Mitigation focus: coordinate embeds and penetrations before closure, reserve lift and scaffold zones, and gate dry-in and fireproofing releases by inspection.
Key interfaces: Foundations / rebar / embeds | Structural steel / deck | Masonry | Roofing | Waterproofing | Facade / glazing | Fireproofing
4. Interior Framing and Core Rough-In
Interior framing, door systems, misc. metals, and elevators depend on dimensional control, early rough-in coordination, and protected access routes.
Mitigation focus: confirm openings, soffits, door prep, elevator feeds, and finish elevations before framing and closure lock the space.
Key interfaces: Interior framing | Doors / frames / hardware | Stairs / railings | Elevators
5. MEP / Life Safety Rough-In
Plumbing, HVAC, duct, process piping, sprinkler, electrical, low voltage, fire alarm, security, and BAS all converge in limited above-ceiling space.
Typical pressure points: duct and pipe route priority, cable tray ownership, sprinkler head locations, device placement, shutdown windows, startup sequencing.
Mitigation focus: run overhead coordination by zone, define pathway ownership, freeze device locations early, and align startup with controls integration.
Key interfaces: Plumbing | HVAC / ductwork | Piping | Fire protection | Electrical | Low voltage / fire alarm | Security | BAS
6. Close-In / Finish Trades
Insulation, firestopping, drywall, ceilings, flooring, tile, coatings, millwork, partitions, and final trim work compete with late rough-in and punch access.
Typical pressure points: incomplete MEP, penetrations after close-in, access panels, moisture-sensitive finishes, finish protection, late startup/testing demands.
Mitigation focus: use closure checklists, enforce firestop completion, protect finished surfaces, and sequence startup/testing with finish completion.
Key interfaces: Drywall / ceilings | Flooring / tile / paint | Millwork / specialties | Final electrical / plumbing / HVAC / fire alarm
7. Exterior / Final Completion
Fence and gate changes, signage, cleaning, commissioning, and owner turnover often collide with final punch, testing, training, and access control changes.
Mitigation focus: preserve emergency access, manage punch by zone, and gate move-in on life-safety approval, startup readiness, and owner training completion.
Key interfaces: Site fencing / gates | Signage | Cleaning / punch | Commissioning / startup | Owner move-in / turnover
